commit.message:
fix: guarantee prebuffer EOF sentinel and abort on prebuffer error

1. EOF sentinel (None) was silently dropped when the prebuffer queue
   was full — put_nowait(None) suppressed by QueueFull. Now makes room
   by discarding one data chunk before placing the sentinel, preventing
   _yield_from_prebuffer() from blocking forever.

2. After draining a failed prebuffer, get_audio_stream fell through to
   _signal_track_completion() — treating a CDN/HTTP error as natural
   track end, desyncing Ynison queue state. Now checks prebuffer.error
   after the yield loop and aborts instead of signaling completion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add systemd user service installer script

install.sh:
- Checks node/npm/systemctl are available
- Copies secrets.example.json -> secrets.json if missing
- Runs npm install --omit=dev
- Writes ~/.config/systemd/user/web-notifications.service with
  the correct WorkingDirectory and ExecStart paths
- Enables and starts the service via systemctl --user
- Enables linger so the service survives logout

commit.message:
feat: scale terminal to fit window while preserving aspect ratio (#32)

* feat: scale terminal to fit window while preserving aspect ratio

Measures natural dimensions at base 16px font-size on mount, then
computes a scale factor from the available viewport (minus 32px
padding on each side) and applies it as a dynamic font-size. All
sizing (80ch widths, 1.4em row heights, input field widths in ch)
inherits from the container font-size so everything scales uniformly.
Padding and status-line margins converted to em units so they scale
proportionally too. Resize listener keeps the terminal fitted when
the browser window changes size.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01U4Nxec54tKj4RuirBsTmkS

* fix: wait for document.fonts.ready before capturing natural terminal dimensions

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Fredric/AS500/sessions/d401d4d6-bc41-4c47-8f4f-a2408c02b100

commit.message:
fix: scope EmployeeNumber unique constraint to TenantId (#19)

The EmployeeNumber unique index was global, preventing different tenants
from having the same employee number. Changed to a composite unique index
on (EmployeeNumber, TenantId) so each tenant has its own number space.

commit.message:
Show elapsed time immediately on reconnect (not after 30 seconds)

When reconnecting to a running scan, users now see the elapsed time
immediately instead of waiting for the first 30-second progress update.

Changes:
- reconnectToRunningExperiment() now displays elapsed time on entry
- Shows message like '[Web] Scan in progress — elapsed 45s (est. 4-5 min remaining)'
- Calculated from createdAt timestamp provided by API
- Does not wait for pollCount % 6 check (first progress interval)

Impact:
- Users get immediate feedback on elapsed time when reconnecting
- More transparent progress on page reload
- Matches expected behavior of 45 seconds showing as 'elapsed 45s'

Testing:
- Start scan, wait 45 seconds
- Refresh page and select repo
- Should immediately show 'elapsed 45s' instead of just '[Info] messages'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: deploy PHC-optimized playbook (Sonnet 4.6 → 0.9718)

Playbook optimized via prompt hill climbing:
- Proposer: Sonnet 4.6 (single-shot mutation)
- Executor: Gemma 4 26B (LM Studio)
- Score: 0.895 → 0.972 (+8.6%)

Key improvements targeting 6 weak cases:
- queen_dispatch: 0.30→1.00 (torrent vs queen disambiguation)
- first_time_setup: 0.60→1.00 (welcome before init)
- activity_investigation: 0.55→0.95 (log/daily before rescue)
- dependency_chain: 0.65→0.95 (depends mandatory before merge-order)
- explain_suspicious: 0.63→0.95 (explain first, no queen/colony mention)
- proactive_advise: 0.63→0.93 (advise as entry command)

Added SCENARIO 13B for suspicious branch investigation.
Expanded decision tree with 6 new entries.

commit.message:
🗃️ feat: spec compliance, bug fixes, and engine rate limiting 🗃️

Spec Compliance (AI.md):
- Remove Makefile clean target (PART 26: exactly 6 targets allowed)
- Add zh/ar/ja translations; remove pt (PART 31: required 7 languages)
- Implement real Valkey/Redis cache via go-redis/v9 (was a stub)
- Add TOTP 2FA API handlers and routes: setup, verify, disable (PART 9)
- Fix 85 inline comment violations across 24 Go files (PART 1)
- Update PART 3 project info: site, repo, description, features
- Add site.txt for OFFICIALSITE LDFLAGS embed
Bug Fixes:
- Fix thumbnail URL in nojs template: /thumbnail to /proxy/thumbnails
- Add engine rate-limit cooldown: on HTTP 429, block engine for 60s
(respects Retry-After header); IsAvailable() gates all dispatches
- Add RateLimitedUntil and IsRateLimited to EngineHealthStats model
Proactive Engine Throttling:
- Add engine_request_interval (ms) to SearchConfig with per-engine
overrides via engine_request_intervals map
- Enforce minimum interval between outbound requests per engine in
MakeRequestWithMod() using a per-engine mutex and lastRequestAt
- Waits are context-aware (cancelled if search context expires)
- Prevents triggering engine rate limits before they happen
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: document acceptance — propagate status + realtime notifications

Migration 126: DB trigger on signature_requests status change
- When status = 'completed' → UPDATE cost_estimates.status = 'accepted'
- When status = 'rejected'  → UPDATE cost_estimates.status = 'rejected'
- When contract completed   → UPDATE contracts.status = 'signed'
- INSERT operator_notification (client_approval_response) on every decision

useEstimates: add Supabase Realtime subscription on cost_estimates
- Contractor sees status change within 1-2s (no page refresh needed)

useOperatorNotifications: add Supabase Realtime subscription
- Bell icon badge updates instantly when client accepts/rejects

commit.message:
Crash simulation, upgrade design, SF failover docs

- Add KvPod::crash()/restart() and SqlitePod::crash()/restart() for
  abrupt pod termination simulation (abort tasks, no graceful shutdown)
- Add test_primary_crash_failover_rejoin: crash primary, failover,
  restart old primary, rejoin as secondary via copy protocol
- Design KvClusterApi crash_pod/restart_pod for reconciler-level tests
- Add rolling-upgrade-design.md with SF/CNPG research and SoT review
- Add E1 (zombie replica) and E2 (switchover catchup) to design-gaps.md
- Add SF SwapPrimary action sequence and Rust trait double-catchup docs
  to failover.md
- Document OpenMode::Existing gap in kvstore and sqlite design docs

commit.message:
fix: add JsonStringEnumConverter to Employee enums for proper JSON deserialization (#17)

The API was rejecting employee creation requests because EmploymentStatus
and EmploymentType enums could not deserialize from JSON string values.
Added [JsonConverter(typeof(JsonStringEnumConverter))] to both enums,
matching the existing pattern used by UserRole.

commit.message:
feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities (#31)

* feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities

- Introduced SceneDiagnostics struct to track entity counts and lifecycle events within scenes.
- Added new fields for tracking total entities, mesh, sprite, text, and physics entity counts, as well as lifecycle counters (created, removed, enter, exit, pause, resume).
- Updated Scene class to maintain diagnostics data during entity addition and removal.
- Enhanced ScriptCommand to support new assertion fields for scene diagnostics, including entity counts and lifecycle events.
- Updated InputScript parsing to handle new assertion types for scenes and global scene counts.
- Implemented variable reference support in assertions for dynamic value comparisons.
- Added comprehensive smoke tests to validate new diagnostics features and assertion functionality.
- Updated existing tests to incorporate new diagnostics checks and ensure backward compatibility.

* fix: correct isFocused diagnostics and unknown-field assertion handling

fix: cascade delete recovery + timeout + input validation

T2-06: ModelsPage stale closures — verified ALREADY SAFE (all
closures use stable React setters/refs, no stale state).

T3-01: sync_engine auth thrashing — verified ALREADY SAFE
(exponential backoff already at L1034-1035).

T3-02: _active_config race — verified ALREADY SAFE (both
read L181 and write L319 use _config_lock).

T3-03: DataManagementPage cascade delete now uses best-effort
error recovery — each step wrapped in safe() helper, partial
errors logged but cleanup continues. Errors recorded in audit.

T3-04: SystemHealthPage GitHub timeout standardized 8s → 10s.

T4-01: camera.py validates source type (int/str), converts
digit strings to int, raises ValueError on invalid type.

T4-02: engine_builder.py sha256_file checks file existence
before attempting to hash.

T138f: Checks _run_send() bot.send_message contains a valid Google Sheets URL

commit.message:
website: add @docusaurus/faster to fix build failure with future.v4 enabled (#451)

Docusaurus 3.10.0 requires `@docusaurus/faster` to be explicitly
installed when `future: { v4: true }` is set in `docusaurus.config.ts`,
but the package was missing from dependencies, causing `docusaurus
build` to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

## Changes

- **`website/package.json`**: add `@docusaurus/faster: 3.10.0` under
`dependencies`
- **`website/package-lock.json`**: updated lockfile to reflect the new
dependency

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add PM agent with brainstorming process

- Testability is the tiebreaker between approaches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor: Establish package definitions as single source of truth (#8)

* Refactor: Establish package definitions as single source of truth

Implements Package first-class citizen architecture with the following changes:

1. Created modules/package-definitions/ structure:
   - Centralized package definitions with metadata, settings, and backend references
   - Sample implementations for git, hello, and hyprland packages
   - Each definition contains: packageId, metadata, defaultSettings, backends

2. Refactored catalog system to derive from definitions:
   - modules/packages/catalog/home.nix now merges definitions with legacy entries
   - modules/packages/catalog/system.nix now merges definitions with legacy entries
   - Definitions take precedence over legacy hardcoded entries
   - Allows gradual migration to definition system

3. Refactored projection registry to derive from definitions:
   - modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/default.nix loads definitions
   - Backend implementation paths derived from definition references
   - Maintains backward compatibility with legacy registry entries

4. Added comprehensive tests:
   - tests/package-definitions.nix validates definition structure and derivation
   - Tests verify metadata, settings, backend paths, and catalog integration

5. Updated documentation:
   - docs/package-metadata.md now explains single source of truth architecture
   - Documented package definition structure and workflow

This maintains all existing boundaries (User/Host/Relation model, no top-level
profile.packages, no new PackageBinding objects) while enabling single-point
package management for future additions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Yttehs-HDX <72240633+Yttehs-HDX@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Phase 1 package definitions - corrections for mergeable PR

Addresses feedback to create a focused Phase 1 implementation:

1. Fixed hello package inconsistency:
   - hello uses crossPlatformUserPackage (home-only)
   - Tests now correctly verify it's NOT in system catalog
   - Removed incorrect assumption it should be cross-scope

2. Implemented auto-discovery for package definitions:
   - Loader now uses builtins.readDir to auto-discover subdirectories
   - Adding new package requires only creating the directory + default.nix
   - No manual registration needed in loader

3. Removed defaultSettings from definitions:
   - Not yet wired into normalize/context/projectionInputs pipeline
   - Removed from git, hello, hyprland definitions
   - Updated tests to not assert on defaultSettings
   - Cleaner Phase 1 scope

4. Added behavioral tests:
   - Test registry derivation from definitions
   - Verify projectors are callable functions
   - Goes beyond structural tests to validate integration

5. Updated documentation to match actual completion:
   - Changed to \"Phase 1\" framing throughout
   - Explicitly listed unimplemented capabilities
   - Removed claims about defaultSettings runtime integration
   - Added note about auto-discovery mechanism

No semantic changes to package metadata or existing catalog entries.
This is a focused, mergeable Phase 1 implementation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Yttehs-HDX <72240633+Yttehs-HDX@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Phase 1 package definitions - corrections for mergeable PR

Addresses all blockers for Phase 1 merge:

1. Fixed projection registry import path:
   - Changed from ../../../package-definitions to ../../../../package-definitions
   - Corrects path from modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/

2. Fixed hello package to support both home and system scopes:
   - hello is used in BOTH user.packages AND host.packages in tests
   - Changed from crossPlatformUserPackage to explicit cross-scope metadata
   - Now supports all targets (home + system) as host-owned package
   - Tests verify hello appears in both home and system catalogs
   - Added assertHelloCrossScope test to validate dual-scope semantics

3. Reverted pipewire semantic changes:
   - owner: host (was incorrectly changed to user)
   - requiresDesktop: false (was incorrectly changed to true)
   - Restored original unsupportedReason/unsupportedSuggestion text
   - No unrelated package semantics modified in this PR

4. Documented lib=builtins limitation:
   - Explicitly noted in Phase 1 limitations
   - Definitions should not depend on lib helpers currently
   - Will be improved in future phase

5. Updated documentation to accurate Phase 1 scope:
   - Listed three migrated packages: git, hyprland, hello
   - Clarified hello as cross-scope example
   - Documented lib limitation
   - Clear separation of implemented vs future capabilities

6. Tests now validate real semantics:
   - assertHelloCrossScope validates dual-scope support
   - assertSystemCatalogInclusions correctly expects hello
   - assertHomeCatalogDerivation validates all three packages
   - All tests reflect actual repository usage patterns

Phase 1 Complete Scope:
✅ Package definition engineering single source of truth
✅ Auto-discovery loader (builtins.readDir)
✅ Catalog derivation from definitions
✅ Projection registry derivation
✅ Three sample migrations with different patterns
✅ Behavioral + structural tests
✅ Hybrid system (definitions + legacy coexist)

Phase 1 Limitations (for future phases):
❌ lib helper support in definitions
❌ defaultSettings runtime integration
❌ Complete package migration
❌ Full definition validation/error reporting

Addresses all remaining blockers for Phase 1 merge:

1. Fixed projection registry import path:
   - Changed from ../../../package-definitions to ../../../../package-definitions
   - Corrects path from modules/projection/backends/home-manager/packages/

2. Fixed hello package to support both home and system scopes:

   - No unrelated package semantics modified in this PR

4. Documented lib=builtins limitation:

   - Clarified hello as cross-scope host-owned example
   - Documented lib limitation and hybrid mode
   - Corrected catalog/registry descriptions to match hybrid implementation
   - Fixed warning behavior description (triggers when not in ANY catalog)

6. Tests now validate real semantics:

   - assertSystemCatalogInclusions correctly expects hello
   - assertHomeCatalogDerivation validates all three packages
   - All tests reflect actual repository usage patterns

Phase 1 Complete Scope:

✅ Hybrid projection registry (definitions + legacy coexist)
✅ Three sample migrations: git (user/home), hyprland (desktop), hello (cross-scope/host)

Phase 1 Limitations (for future phases):

commit.message:
fix: CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID empty string now falls back to default sheet ID

os.environ.get() with a default only works when var is UNSET.
When bot.env contains 'CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID=' (empty value), Python returns
empty string, causing URL 'spreadsheets/d//edit#gid=0'. Fixed with 'or'.
Also documented CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID in bot.env.template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: release v1.0.9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): cv2.imwrite return check + chdir error handling

T2-01: _save_prediction_image now checks cv2.imwrite return value.
On failure, cleans up partial file and returns None. Caller in
predict() guards os.unlink with 'if image_path' to prevent
TypeError on None.

T2-03: main.py os.chdir() wrapped in try/except. On failure,
prints clear fatal error to stderr and exits immediately instead
of continuing with broken relative paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
